contracts.transformation: Project identity, local quality authority, Campaign definition, state path, and validation entrypoints are indexed without executing the project.
preserved_rules: Project-local mandatory rules remain authoritative over shared defaults.
Data-mutating commands remain outside read-only mapping.
contracts.freshness_rule: All listed sources must resolve at the frozen source commit.

contracts.transformation: Mandatory repository rules and accepted Phase 2 scope are combined while keeping explicit-plan boundaries intact.
preserved_rules: Business behavior, database schema, deployment, authentication, and destructive routes require an explicit plan.
Phase 2 acceptance does not authorize later project phases.
gaps: Phase authority is distributed across repository rules, Campaign scope, and acceptance evidence.
contracts.freshness_rule: Scope and acceptance evidence must refer to the same Phase 2 Campaign generation.

contracts.transformation: Mandatory safety rules, unsafe command categories, exact task allowlists, and Campaign stop policies map with deny-unknown behavior.
preserved_rules: Secrets are never printed.
Database imports, migrations, seeds, and E2E remain data-mutating unless explicitly authorized.
Task allowlists do not expand into repository-wide write permission.
gaps: There is no standalone normalized capability policy artifact.
contracts.freshness_rule: Repository rules and Campaign policy must be read at the frozen commit.

contracts.transformation: No role projection is permitted from generic coding-agent guidance or Campaign task labels.
gaps: The project has no explicit role contract defining accepted inputs, outputs, capabilities, self-approval, and escalation conditions.
contracts.freshness_rule: A future role contract requires an explicit project artifact and separate approval.

contracts.transformation: Campaign task IDs, exact allowed and changed paths, budgets, validation commands, and initial statuses map directly.
preserved_rules: Changed paths must stay within each task allowlist.
Secret and scope violations stop the Campaign.
contracts.freshness_rule: Campaign definition and runner semantics must come from the same frozen commit.

contracts.transformation: Per-task changed-file and elapsed-time ceilings plus Campaign maximum attempts map directly.
preserved_rules: A task cannot exceed its declared changed-file or time budget.
Maximum attempts remain two for the accepted Phase 2 Campaign.
contracts.freshness_rule: Budget values must bind to the exact Campaign definition used by the runner.

contracts.transformation: Required check results, Campaign validation, task outcomes, and historical debt are indexed without turning accepted scope into a globally clean baseline.
preserved_rules: Historical ESLint violations remain visible as locked baseline debt.
Warnings and exposed-key rotation risk remain visible after acceptance.
gaps: Gate evidence is primarily prose rather than a single normalized machine-readable result.
contracts.freshness_rule: Verification dates, command counts, and Campaign evidence must retain their original snapshot identity.

contracts.transformation: Ready, completed, failed, and blocked task states plus persisted resume behavior map directly; ignored local state is not promoted to committed truth.
preserved_rules: A failed task blocks the Campaign and persists state for resume.
The generated state file remains local and ignored by git.
The accepted final clean run remains distinct from the earlier intentional failure run.
contracts.freshness_rule: Local ignored state must never be inferred from committed acceptance prose alone.

contracts.transformation: Acceptance prose is not converted into a portable approval fingerprint.
gaps: No machine-readable approval record binds decision, approver, scope, source commit, and artifact fingerprints.
contracts.freshness_rule: A future approval record requires an explicit immutable project artifact.

contracts.transformation: Secret detection, scope violations, budget failures, validation failures, and explicit-plan requirements are indexed as distinct escalation reasons.
preserved_rules: Secret and scope violations stop execution.
Data mutation and protected business changes require explicit authority.
Retry and resume do not erase the original failure evidence.
gaps: No standalone escalation artifact defines a portable safe-resume fingerprint.
contracts.freshness_rule: Escalation reasons must remain tied to the originating task attempt and Campaign state.

contracts.transformation: Baseline, acceptance narrative, Campaign definition, and local state location form a read-only evidence index; source artifacts remain authoritative.
preserved_rules: Accepted Phase 2 scope coexists with visible historical baseline debt.
Ignored local state is not represented as committed evidence.
Secret remediation evidence never includes the secret value.
gaps: No single committed EvidenceBundle binds the accepted run outputs and fingerprints.
contracts.freshness_rule: Committed evidence and ignored runtime state remain separate evidence classes.

forbidden_inferences: Phase 2 acceptance is not approval for later business or deployment changes.
Ignored local Campaign state is not committed repository truth.
AGENTS.md is project policy, not a human approval record.
Generic coding-agent guidance is not a reusable RoleContract.
